calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Vacating/Set Aside Judgement Due To Extrinsic Fraud/Mistake
Off Calendar Jul-26-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Appearing via telephone Kimberly Sturgell is present.

Kimberly stated that she has no further arguement to add to her motion, that she read the tenative ruling late last night and that she is in the process of getting copies of the bond.

Having heard oral argument and reviewing the court filed documents, the Court adopts the tenative ruiling as follows: The Motion for Order Vacating/Set Aside Judgment Due to Extrinsic Fraud/Mistake is denied. Even assuming it has been properly noticed on all interested parties, which does not appear from the record, Ms. Sturgell has failed to present credible evidence from which the Court could conclude either that the Public Administrator was required to give her notice of the petition to administer the estate or that the Public Administrator committed any fraud on the court in procuring letters of administration or carrying out his duties as administrator. Ms. Sturgell's entitlement to notice depends on her father being dead when these proceedings were instituted. She has offered only speculation that was the case. Even if he had been dead, Ms. Sturgell has offered no evidence that she was known to or readily ascertainable by the Public Administrator. She points to savings bonds she says she received after her aunt's death, but she offers no proof that they were sent by the Public Administrator or even that the Public Administrator was aware of the aunt's practice of sending bonds to her nieces. She points to a power of attorney by her father in favor of a third party, which she claims was forged, but she offers no proof that the Public Administrator knew of any forgery or participated in any scheme to administer the estate on the basis of such a forgery. There is no evidence of any collusion or conspiracy between the Public Administrator and the alleged perpetrator of the alleged fraud, Mr. Morris. The probate petition was duly published in a newspaper of record, giving notice to unknown heirs in accordance with the Probate Code. The other evidence and arguments offered by Ms. Sturgell are irrelevant or go only to the merits of the estate administration orders and could not provide a basis to vacate the earlier judgments. In sum, Ms. Sturgell's motion provides no basis to disturb the finality of the 1999 order issuing testamentary letters to the Public Administrator or the 2001 order approving final distribution of the estate.

Judge Peter J. Busch, Clerk:N. Wong, Bailiff: Deputy Nuque. Not Reported, D501.

Petition To Remove Personal Representative
Off Calendar Jan-17-2017 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kimberly Sturgell appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.

Ms. Sturgell stated that she contacted Attorney Mila Lovovich, that she sent the retaining agreement to Ms. Lovovich, that now Ms. Lovovich is not answering her calls and that she will not be pursuing the petition. The Petition to Remove Personal Representative is taken off calendar at Ms. Sturgell's request.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: D. Duong. Not reported.

Petition To Remove Personal Representative
Off Calendar Nov-29-2016 Continued To Jan-17-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kimberly Sturgell (via court call) in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that Ms. Sturgell was to have obtained counsel regarding relief to be applied. Ms. Sturgell reported that she had been looking, that Milla Lvovich is considering the case and that she is waiting to hear if she will take it. The Court stated that it will grant one more continuance and that either someone needs to appear and file for relief or the matter will be taken off calendar. Ms. Sturgell stated that if Ms. Lvovich does not take the case she will file a motion. The Petition to Remove Personal Representative was continued to January 17, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and the Court stated that something must be on file prior to that date.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

Petition To Remove Personal Representative
Off Calendar Aug-16-2016 Continued To Sep-26-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kim Sturgell; Joseph Sturgell.

Ms. Sturgell stated that she and her sister had never received notice of the petition for probate, and that the judgment on the petition was obtained through extrinsic fraud. She said that the durable power of attorney had been forged, and that neither she nor her sister had received any distribution from the decedent, contrary to the intent of the decedent. The Court replied that there was a final judgment entered in 2001, and that because the judgment was final he lacked jurisdiction to grant any of the relief sought in the petition. The matter was continued to September 27, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204, to give the petitioner time to seek legal counsel or take other appropriate steps.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Chan. (Not reported)
